entry.title: Re: How to Prevent Duplicate GST Numbers in Customer and...
entry.content:
Hi @SivaNagiReddy ,

Yes, if you maintain the GST Number in the Identification tab using the tax number category IN3 (GST Registration Number), SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides a standard option to perform duplicate GST number validation.

Please refer to the following KBAs for detailed information:

KBA 2886187 – How to Activate Tax Number Category Duplication Check for Business Partners in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

KBA 2886241 – How to Change Duplicate Tax Number Category from an Information Message to an Error Message in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

You can use the following SSCUIs:

  • SSCUI 102415 – Maintain Tax Number Categories: To activate duplicate checks for GST numbers.

  • SSCUI 103601 – Change Message Control for Duplicate Tax Numbers: To change the duplicate tax number message from a warning/information message to an error message.

These configurations will help prevent the creation of Business Partners with duplicate GST numbers.

Regards,
Vinith

entry.title: Re: Handling Large Volumes of Data Between SAP CPI and S/4 HANA
entry.content: valuable tips.. Thanks 👍

entry.title: Re: Does the WBS field on the sales order have an extensi...
entry.content:
Did you already read 2733433 - BAdI option to set/change values in standard sales fields in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

  • Try Customer Influence Portal

entry.title: Configuration for Automatic HU Packing during GR for Production Orders
entry.content:
Hi Team,

For finished products, HU packing during Goods Receipt with respect to the Production Order is expected to happen automatically.

Could you please provide the relevant configuration details in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud that control automatic HU packing for this scenario?

Thanks & Regards,
Kiran

entry.title: SAP Fiori Reporting only
entry.content: Implementing SAP Fiori Reporting only, without deploying the broader SAP Fiori application suite - what are the challenges one should be aware of.

entry.title: Re: Behavior of Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation after Clearing
entry.content:
HI dear @KevinSigl 

Thank you for the clarification regarding the statistical posting behavior in Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation.

You mentioned that you have already started analyzing possible scenarios to avoid the statistical posting. Could you please share any updates or findings on this topic?

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Expense and VAT Posting Issue for Credit Ca...
entry.content:
I would avoid posting both documents as if they are two separate purchases.

In this case the card transaction and the supplier tax invoice are evidence for the same underlying spend. If Concur already posted the expense and liability, then creating a normal supplier invoice later can easily double book the expense and input VAT.

I would add a matching step before any supplier invoice is posted from the tax invoice:

match by employee/card holder, supplier, transaction date, amount, currency and tax invoice number.

If there is already a Concur/card entry for the same spend, the tax invoice should either be attached to that existing expense record or handled through a clearing/reclassification process, not booked as a new expense.

If the supplier tax invoice amount, VAT amount, supplier name or date does not match the card transaction cleanly, route it to AP review. But if it does match, the process should preserve the tax invoice for compliance without creating a second expense or duplicate input VAT claim

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 06 - Project specific configuration
Re: Blocked bin don´t permit transfer
entry.content: Hi Chris, have a nice day. The client wants the blocked position to allow only material transfer and not material removal.

entry.title: Re: Subcontracting Planned Order Creation Inquiry
entry.content: Maintain procurement type + special procurement in the material master, run MRP, and convert the planned order. This ensures PLAF-SOBES = 3 is populated and the subcontracting flow works correctly.

entry.title: Re: Joule Studio: From Business Intent to Enterprise-Ready AI Agents
entry.content: As an SAP partner we are already using Joule Studio 2.0, should we do something to get Joule Studio 2.0 design-time access?

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 06 - Project specific configuration
Re: MR21 – New Price Field Greyed Out – Standard Price No...
entry.content:
The MR21 transaction for updating the new standard price is greyed out in a specific plant.

Check Material Ledger Activation

  • Confirm whether the Material Ledger (ML) is active in the plant.
  • Execute SPRO → CKMSTART to activate the Material Ledger if required.

Review Error Messages

  • Check for any system-generated error messages (e.g., CKPRCH 038 indicating missing authorization).

Else provide some information on those materials (type of material, etc.)

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 06 - Project specific configuration
Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 06 - Project specific configuration
CBC Mandatory SD Organizational Units Due to Baseline Accelerator Scope Selection
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are working on an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (3SL) implementation.

During project scoping, the Baseline Accelerator was used, which automatically included several SD-related scope items. However, the customer does not have any SD business processes in scope and does not plan to use SD functionality.

As a result, CBC now requires us to create several SD organizational units (Sales Organization, Distribution Channel, Division, Shipping Points, and related assignments) to complete the Organizational Structure activity.

We attempted to remove the related SD scope items, but CBC no longer allows us to deselect them.

Would there be any functional, technical, or maintenance concerns if we simply create these mandatory organizational units to complete CBC, while leaving them otherwise unused?

Additionally, is there a recommended SAP approach for handling scope items that were automatically selected by the Baseline Accelerator but are not required by the customer?

Thank you in advance for your support.

BR,
Evrim

@Melis_Sorgun 

@MerveBayturk

entry.title: WorkforcePerson Integration from SF to S4 HANA via MDI
entry.content:
We have a Requirement to Store the "Position" in S4 HANA Public Cloud from SF through MDI. In SuccessFactors we have the standard Field called  "Position" and same receiving to MDI. 

SAP GROW don't have any standard "Position" and Created a Custom Field and we are Implemented  the BADI WFD_MDI_EXT_FIELD_MAPPING, its working , whereas our actual Requirement is Standard Read the Field of MDI.

Can somebody help us how to proceed with this requirement?

entry.title: PoC Real in CNE5 w/o values PS
entry.content: What is the exact condition that triggers CNE1 to generate actual POC records? Is there a specific configuration or prerequisite that enables actual POC calculation beyond what is described above?

entry.title: Re: AI across Customer Project processes
entry.content: @Kapil_Kumar01 Thank you for your question. While i am not an integration expert to answer your question, but the perspective of viewing things change when it comes to AI & agents... One could for example use an agent to extract information of the lead and create a project on the basis all the while not having the integration in place..provided the APIs and data is in place..Agents can orchestrate the process and run these steps..

entry.title: Re: Under the Hood of ABAP CDC in SAP Datasphere Replication Flows
entry.content:
Hi @MKreitlein ,

When you pause a replication flow, the S/4HANA system still continue to capture the delta changes and push the changes to the buffer table so that the Datasphere can pull all that delta information from Buffer table once we resume the Replication flow. Post that the processed data will be cleared from the buffer table.

Regards,

Anil Kumar.

entry.title: Re: Your S/4HANA & Ariba implementation is live, but your GRC ruleset do not have Ariba rules co
entry.content:
Dear Madhu,

 

We are in Ariba implementation, plan is use existing GRC 12.0 with extension to  Ariba . 

Now client requirement is we need to control Ariba groups in Purchasing Groups,P,Org,Relse code,Section code level, Plant level,etc..

And also activity level control also they are expecting for ariba groups.

We know, we cannot trim Ariba groups into granule level.

Please suggest if you come across this in your Ariba implementation projects.

entry.title: Meet the AI Layer in SAP Solution Scout
entry.content:
SAP Solution Scout lets you navigate the SAP portfolio by industry value chain—from Business Priorities down to individual Solution Capabilities—and the capability detail view now includes the supporting AI offerings that come with each capability. This means that when you evaluate a capability, you see not only the value proposition, value drivers, and related innovations, but also which AI Assistants and AI Agents are embedded. That’s how Enterprise AI delivers real value—not as a roadmap, but as deployed capability.
This integrated view changes the exploration question from "does this capability cover my requirement?" to "does this capability cover my requirement, and how intelligent is it?"
SAP Solution Scout is self-service and available to SAP customers, partners, and employees—explore at your own pace and build a collection of relevant capabilities and AI offerings. Start exploring at https://solutionscout.sap.com.

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM MFS - Short Dump while starting the communication channel.
entry.content: thank you so much its working fine

entry.title: SAP HANA Cloud, HANA Database: Jump start mission - Database connection command "Knowledge Check"
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

Recently I have completed a course (Mission) from SAP Learning  and done all the steps mentioned there with succesfully passed the "Knowledge Check" Options. The learning pathe is as below. 

 

Jump Start Your SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database > Create a Development Project and Calculation View >
Grant Access to Calculation Views
 

But at the "Knowledge check" of the last section, I have answered the question correctly but it shows the error as below in screenshot. The question was

"Enter the SQL command used to log in as a different user in the SAP HANA Database Explorer." 

I have answered with the command used to connect to the DB "CONNECT report PASSWORD Password1;". But the apps are showing the error "Sorry, that’s not quite right.". 

Need your cooperation to resolve the problem.

 

 

Thanks

Wahid

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors Cybersecurity Übersicht

entry.title: Sales Analysis Report – Incorrect Gross Profit Calculation for Service Items Linked to Resource
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

We are facing an issue in SAP Business One HANA version 10 where the Gross Profit in Sales Analysis Report is showing incorrect values. In this sales order,  Service Items are linked with Resource Master Data.

Kindly let us know how gross profit calculates here. what is formula. 

we renamed menu sales order to work order as per customer requirement. 

In General setting, Gross Profit on selling Price made it. 

Kindly advise how gross profit calculates. here. 

We have attached the screenshot for reference.

 

Thans

entry.title: Re: MR21 – New Price Field Greyed Out – Standard Price No...
entry.content: Check whether Accounting view has been extended to those materials

entry.title: Estratégia de liberação para Requisição originada de serviços de PM
entry.content: Todas as configurações feitas por analista funcional do módulo de Materiais no SAP ECC foram feitas, SPRO, CT04. CL02, CL20N, CL24N, para estratégia liberação para Requisições de compras originadas de ordem de manutenção criadas NA  transação IW31 e após liberação na transação IW32 para Serviços, estes criados na transação AC01 - SAP ECC,  quando liberada na transação IW32 não é gerada ou ativada para a estratégia de liberação Configura.  Feito os testes diretamante na  transação ME51N para a mesma ordem de PM e gera a estratégia.   O que pode ser isso ? Lembrando que ainda não estou envolvendo o ABAP. Apliquei a tabela chave CEBAN e todos os principais campos, tipo de Requisição, quantidade, valor, tipos de classificação de item,  etc, porem não está levando para liberação.  Algumas orientações pediram para fazer com ABAP via BAPI ou aplicar método.  Agradeço sua atenção.

Thanks

Take the First Step Toward a Connected Enterprise

Understanding business networks is no longer optional—it’s essential for navigating today’s digital economy. By completing this course, you’ll gain valuable insights into how organizations collaborate smarter and operate more efficiently.

🚀Start your learning journey today: Discovering Business Network Solutions 

Join the Conversation

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC - Electronic Invoicing for Italy : ItalyReceiveNotification without SNI
entry.content: Thanks. So you have the SAP-supported E2E process if you are also using SAP custom domain. Where does SNI then come into the picture? DRC Italy allows you to connect directly with the SDI platform without needing another third party.

entry.title: Re: ABAP development tools for Visual Studio Code is now available on the VS Code marketplace!
entry.content:
I used Claude to extract this extension and manually added my on‑premise system to test it. It worked, but it doesn’t provide any methods or features to create Function Modules, reports, or database tables. It still basically works like ADT for ABAP Cloud.

At least there’s now an official MCP for ABAP, so I don’t need to use other MCPs from GitHub.

entry.title: Re: Plant language change from EN to JA ( Japanese) for JP00
entry.content:
Recommended Procedure for Language Change in Plant Configuration:

  1. Review SAP Note/KBA 3665722

    • "Unable to change the language of a plant in configuration 'Define Plant'"

  2. Execute Configuration Steps

    • Transaction: OX10
    • SSCUI: 105877
    • Navigate to Address/Communication Data to modify the plant language.

  3. Validate Customized Forms & Messages

    • Ensure all developed/customized forms and messages support the new language setting.
    • Check also for master data long text language 

Note: Failure to update dependent objects may result in inconsistencies.

entry.title: Variable Pay: how to correctly configure field formats for Business Goals
entry.content:
Hi,

I have configured business goals, but the percentage for Goal Section Weight still displays 600% instead of 60%

the business Section Amount should be 5310 EUR, instead of 53103.78

for business goals the amount should be divided by 10 and rounded up.

for example, the value: 15931,13 / 10 = 1593 EUR

can you please give me an advice how to set it up?

I tried several formats in Set Number Format Rules and than used them for specific field in Set Number Formats, but I don´t seem to get it right.
thank you in advance

entry.title: Re: Behind the Curtains - what's new about Maintenance Certificates

*** ERROR => DlLoadLib()==DLENOACCESS - dlopen("/usr/sap//SYS/exe/run/libsapcrypto.so") FAILED
"/usr/sap//SYS/exe/run/libsapcrypto.so: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory" [dlux_mt.c 445]
*** ERROR => SapSecuInit: could not load libsapsecu >/usr/sap//SYS/exe/run/libsapcrypto.so< [secuload.c 554]

Is there a solution?

entry.title: PS certifications Public edition
entry.content:
Hello Gurus

Can you help me with the list of certification codes or resources for PS (Project System) on S4 HANA Public Cloud edition?

entry.title: SAP HANA Cloud Activation on SAP BTP and Database Operations Using JDBC from Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Introduction

While working on integration scenarios in SAP BTP, there are many cases where SAP Cloud Integration needs to directly read from or write to a database. SAP HANA Cloud is the obvious choice on BTP, and the JDBC adapter in Cloud Integration makes this integration straightforward—once the setup is clear.

In this blog, I will walk through:

  • How to activate SAP HANA Cloud in SAP BTP
  • How to create a simple database structure (schema, table, user)
  • How to connect SAP Cloud Integration to HANA Cloud using JDBC
  • How to perform basic SELECT, INSERT, and UPDATE operations from an iFlow

The focus of this blog is mainly on the enablement and configuration steps, because that is where most issues usually occur.

You can read my previous blogs here on SAP TMS and SAP ANS Integration with Cloud Integration

Content Transport Using SAP Cloud Transport Management Service for SAP CI CF Environment

Setting Up Alerts for SAP Cloud Integration Using SAP Alert Notification Service 

 

Prerequisites

Before starting, make sure you have:

  • Access to an SAP BTP Global Account
  • A Subaccount (Cloud Foundry environment)
  • SAP Integration Suite enabled
  • Required roles to:
    • Create HANA Cloud instances
    • Access HANA Database Explorer
    • Configure Cloud Integration

Step 1: Enable SAP HANA Cloud in SAP BTP

Check Entitlements

First, ensure SAP HANA Cloud is entitled:

  1. Go to BTP Cockpit ->Global Account ->Entitlements

Check if SAP HANA Cloud is available and assign quota to your subaccount if needed

If the entitlement is missing, you won’t be able to create a HANA Cloud instance.

Subscribe SAP HANA Cloud Tools application

  1. Navigate to Subaccount -> Instances and Subscriptions ->Create

Select Service: SAP HANA Cloud and Plan: tools

 

Create SAP HANA Cloud Instance

  1. Navigate to Subaccount ->Instances and Subscriptions->Create

Select Service: SAP HANA Cloud and Plan: hana
Provide Instance name (example: hana-cloud-ci-db) and System password

Create the instance , provisioning usually takes 20–30 minutes, so be patient here.

Verify Database Status

Once the instance is created:

Assign below roles to your user to open and perform operations in HANA Cloud 

Navigate to Subaccount ->Security->Users ->Assign Roles

  1. Open SAP HANA Cloud from the subaccount 
Make sure the database status is Running

Step 2: Create Database Objects

Open HANA Database Explorer

  1. From HANA Cloud Central->Open Database Explorer
Log in using the admin user (DBADMIN) 
\
Select SQL Console

Create Schema and Table

Create a schema first:

CREATE SCHEMA CI_DEMO_SCHEMA;

Now create a simple table:

CREATE TABLE CI_DEMO_SCHEMA.EMPLOYEE (

    EMP_ID INTEGER NOT NULL,

    EMP_NAME NVARCHAR(100),

    DEPARTMENT NVARCHAR(50),

    SALARY DECIMAL(15, 2),

    PRIMARY KEY (EMP_ID)

);

 

Insert some test data:

INSERT INTO CI_DEMO_SCHEMA.EMPLOYEE

VALUES (1, 'ABC DEF', 'IT', 90000);

 

Create a Technical User for Integration
Do not use DBADMIN for integration access.

CREATE USER CI_JDBC_USER PASSWORD "Pas****@1**3";
GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE ON SCHEMA CI_DEMO_SCHEMA TO CI_JDBC_USER;

This user will be used later in Cloud Integration.

Step 3: Configure JDBC Connectivity in Cloud Integration

Get JDBC Connection Details

From HANA Cloud Central, note the host by Copy SQL Endpoint

98dd5c42*****************0d8792.hna1.prod-us10.hanacloud.ondemand.com:443

HANA Cloud JDBC URL format required  as per below :

jdbc:sap://<host>:443/?encrypt=true and Port 443 is used with SSL.

The complete URL will be become as per below :

jdbc:sap://98dd5c42*****************0d8792.hna1.prod-us10.hanacloud.ondemand.com:443/?encrypt=true

Navigate to Integration Suite->Security ->Manage JDBC Data Source and maintain as per below 

Configure JDBC Adapter in iFlow

HTTPS Sender to Trigger message from Postman and JDBC Receiver 

In JDBC connection maintain the JDBC Data Source name created in previous step.

Step 4: Testing

1. Select Operation 

Configure the Postman to trigger CI iflow to trigger the HTTP endpoint

SELECT * FROM CI_DEMO_SCHEMA.EMPLOYEE;
 

2. Insert Operation

<root>
    <insert_statement>
        <dbTableName action="INSERT">
            <table>CI_DEMO_SCHEMA.EMPLOYEE</table>
            <access>
                <EMP_ID>2</EMP_ID>
                <EMP_NAME>GGG HHHH</EMP_NAME>
                <DEPARTMENT>SAP</DEPARTMENT>
                <SALARY>99000.00</SALARY>
            </access>
        </dbTableName>
    </insert_statement>
</root>

 

Perform select again to view the inserted record 

3. Update operation :

<root>
    <update_statement>
        <dbTableName action="UPDATE">
            <table>CI_DEMO_SCHEMA.EMPLOYEE</table>
            <access>
                <EMP_NAME>NNN JJJ</EMP_NAME>
            </access>
            <key>
                <EMP_ID>2</EMP_ID>
            </key>
        </dbTableName>
    </update_statement>
</root>


Perform select again to view updated record 
Table from HABA Cloud DB 

Summary 

This POC highlighted several common pitfalls when working with SAP HANA Cloud and the JDBC adapter in SAP Cloud Integration. Most issues were not related to connectivity, but rather to user security policies, JDBC execution modes, and strict XML structure requirements. Understanding these early helps avoid hours of debugging.

Reference 

JDBC Receiver Adapter Payload Operations

SAP HANA Cloud

entry.title: Re: Blocked bin don´t permit transfer
entry.content:
Hi Carlos,

In standard EWM blocking a bin for putaway means that no materials can be put into this storage bin.
While block for stock removal means that no materials can be removed from this storage bin.

Regards,
João

entry.title: Current vs Non-Current Deferred Revenue Handling in EBRR – Standard Capabilities in PublicCloud 2602
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a requirement to classify deferred revenue generated via Event-Based Revenue Recognition (EBRR) into current and non-current portions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

In this context, we have the following queries:

  1. Reclassification to Separate G/L Accounts
    In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602, is there any standard functionality to reclassify EBRR-managed deferred revenue into current and non-current G/L accounts at period end (e.g., via automated postings), without requiring manual journal entries on EBRR-managed accounts?

  2. Standard Reporting for Current vs Non-Current Split
    Is there any standard analytical report that provides a current vs non-current classification of EBRR-generated deferred revenue balances across:

    • Sales Orders
    • Projects
    • Service Documents

As we are not using Contract-Based Revenue Recognition, the standard report “Contract Balance Reclassification” is not applicable in our scenario.

Appreciate your guidance on standard capabilities or recommended approaches in Public Cloud.

entry.title: Re: Why SAP S/4HANA Transformations Fail When Business Processes Are Automated Before They Are Optim
entry.content: Great insights and perspective.

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC - Electronic Invoicing for Italy : ItalyReceiveNotification without SNI
entry.content: Yes, we use a custom domain, but when Italy tries to call, for example, test.sdi.mycompany.com/cxf/ItalyReceiveNotification, it fails with an error 500 and we suspect that without SNI their request arrives at https://api.cf.eu20-001.hana.ondemand.com with the incorrect certificate.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business one Backup restoration warning
entry.content:
Hi ,

“Could you please let us know if you was able to resolve the issue?”

entry.title: Re: Is It Okay to Use Only RECN Instead of ABST for Recon...
entry.content:
Hi Minsung_Lee,

To answer your question directly: From my perspective, there are no critical system integration or long-term issues when using exclusively the RECN account group instead of ABST.

The difference between ABST and RECN in the standard SAP pre-delivered Chart of Accounts (YCOA) is not a hard architectural restriction, but rather a matter of Master Data Governance (Field Status configuration).

Here is the factual breakdown of the impact (check SAP Help Account Groups )

1. The Difference: Field Status In S/4HANA Cloud, Account Groups primarily control number ranges and the Field Status of G/L master data.

  • ABST is configured to hide the "Recon. Account Ready for Input" flag.

  • RECN is configured to make the "Recon. Account Ready for Input" flag optional/visible in the company code specific data.

If you assign all reconciliation accounts to RECN but leave the "Ready for Input" checkbox unticked, the accounts will behave exactly like standard ABST accounts.

2. Impact on your specific concerns:

  • Integration issues between subledger (S/L) and general ledger (G/L): None. Setting an account to RECN and even checking the "Ready for Input" flag does not open the account to direct FI journal entries. Because the account is still defined as a reconciliation account (for Customers or Suppliers), S/4HANA will strictly block direct manual postings (e.g., via "Post General Journal Entries"). All postings must still flow through the subledger, meaning S/L and G/L balances will remain perfectly aligned.

  • Restrictions when using Alternative Reconciliation Account: Enabler, not a restriction. Using RECN is actually a prerequisite for using Alternative Reconciliation Accounts. If a business process requires a user to manually change the reconciliation account during document entry (e.g., in "Create Incoming Invoices"), the targeted G/L account must be in the RECN group with the "Ready for Input" flag checked. Check Sap Note 2844132 

     

  • Impact on Special G/L Indicator processing: None. Special G/L transactions (like Down Payments) use their own separate logic for account determination (Automatic Account determination app.). The system looks at this configuration for Special G/L indicators to find the target alternative reconciliation account. Even RECN Account Group opens the alternative reconciliation account filed during manual posting, you can not save a different target account for the same reconciliation account in the configuration. Therefore, there is no effect as well.

  • Effects on standard reports or analytics tools: Minimal (Process-dependent). The system standard reporting remains unaffected. The only potential "pitfall" is human error. If you enable the "Ready for Input" flag on all RECN accounts, users might accidentally change the reconciliation account manually during regular invoice posting when they shouldn't. This won't break subledger integration, but it could misclassify balances on your Trial Balance (e.g., classifying standard trade payables under a different liability bucket).

Conclusion: Using only RECN is completely fine and will not break the system. However, from a strict Master Data Governance perspective, it is best practice to keep the "Ready for Input" flag unticked unless the specific account explicitly requires manual alternative reconciliation postings.

Hope it helps.

Best Regards,

Michal

entry.title: How Can You Position SAP Business Network Solutions with Confidence?
entry.content:
The “Positioning SAP Solutions for Business Network Solutions for Partners” course is designed to help you do exactly that—build confidence, sharpen your messaging, and understand where SAP Business Network fits in real-world scenarios.

 

Knowing how to position SAP Business Network solutions helps you connect these needs to the right offerings—and that’s where this course comes in. 
This course moves beyond basics and focuses on real-world positioning skills for partners.

You’ll explore:

  • How to identify customer needs and business challenges
  • Where SAP Business Network solutions fit into different scenarios
  • How to articulate value clearly and effectively
  • Key messaging to position solutions confidently in conversations

It’s practical, relevant, and directly applicable to your day-to-day work.

This learning journey is ideal for:

  • SAP partners and consultants
  • Pre-sales and sales professionals
  • Anyone involved in solution positioning and customer engagement

If your role involves explaining or recommending SAP solutions—this course will elevate your impact.

You can start today and immediately apply what you learn

Join the discussion

How do you position SAP Business Network solutions in your projects or conversations? Share your insights or questions in the comments—we’d love to hear from you!

entry.title: Re: Procurement of Services in SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Latest Guidance on Services Procurement Solutions

For latest guidance on services procurement solutions, kindly refer links below.

  • SAP Fieldglass and SAP Ariba solutions: Services procurement feature comparison

entry.title: Re: Unable to Assign House Bank Account ID (GIRO) in S/4H...

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud - FAQs
entry.content: @ChanchalGupta - Please reach out to your account AE/CSM for the commercial licensing. At high-level you need BDC core components subscription + LoB Intelligent Apps/Content subscription.

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC - Electronic Invoicing for Italy : ItalyReceiveNotification without SNI
entry.content: Thanks, but what is SNI's role in this process?

entry.title: Re: SAP Energy Park: Launch of New Use Cases
entry.content: Hi Palvar, Thanks for letting us know. I fixed the link to the video. Enjoy!

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 06 - Project specific configuration
Re: SAP DataSphere | Asking about CU Limitation and Notif...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

In Datasphere, we have a capacities monitoring tool that offers insights into the monthly and daily unit consumption. You can find more details about this in the help page https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/9f804b8efa8043539289f42f372c4862/ba3d05baac854171914c09d64bed7202.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST . While there isn't a CU limitation or alert regarding the consumption at the moment, you're welcome to create a feature request in the influence channel, Influence Opportunity Homepage - Customer Influence , to propose an enhancement.

Best regards,
Joana

entry.title: Re: Offline document upload and download in SSAM 2310
entry.content: It normally will, but since you are using Open Text I do not know if will have any impact on the document attachment process.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the Data Professionals Community
entry.content:
@Daniel_Dukes 

This is a welcoming and useful starting point for the Data Professionals Community. I like that it clearly points people toward discussions, blog posts, and shared lessons learned, because that makes the community feel practical and collaborative from the start. It seems like a good space for people who want to learn from real implementation experience and stay connected to others in the field.

entry.title: Re: Serial number converting in to special character
entry.content:
Since we cannot directly access your system, we recommend the following steps to diagnose and resolve the issue of serial numbers being converted to special characters during upload:

  • Verify & Correct Excel File Encoding 
    Excel files ( .xlsx, .xls) may use different encodings (UTF-8, ANSI, etc.), which can lead to data corruption when processed by SAP. To mitigate this, save fime in utf-8 (or the appropriate encoding for your region)..
  • Confirm SAP System Encoding Settings
    SAP may misinterpret input data due to incorrect system-level encoding configurations. To ensure proper handling: Ensure the system is configured to UTF-8 (or the appropriate encoding for your region).
  • Additional Checks (Method-Dependent)
    Further troubleshooting steps depend on the upload method

entry.title: Re: Live Versions for Planning Models in SAP Analytics Cloud: Microsoft Azure Is Here!
entry.content:
@Max_Gander 

This is a meaningful step for live planning because it expands the supported source landscape and makes SAC planning models more flexible for customers on Azure. I like that the article explains both how to create a live model and how to add a live version to an existing one, since that makes the feature feel immediately usable rather than theoretical. It seems especially valuable for teams that want live access to source data without giving up planning capabilities.

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC - Electronic Invoicing for Italy : ItalyReceiveNotification without SNI
entry.content: Explanation I receive : The Italian Revenue Agency's *Sistema di Interscambio* (SdI) client does not send the SNI extension during the TLS handshake. When the SdI makes the `ItalyReceiveNotification` call to SAP BTP, the suite expects the host name indication (SNI) during the TLS handshake. Because it is not received, the SAP BTP load balancer or Ingress controller rejects the connection or fails to route it correctly at the TLS level, resulting in an internal HTTP 500 error (Internal Server Error).

entry.title: Re: Improved Data Access in Stories: Introducing the Data Panel and Drag & Drop Creation of Char
entry.content:
@Tunir 

This is a welcome usability improvement for SAC stories because it makes data access feel more direct and less dependent on the Builder Panel. I like that the new Data Panel can be used independently with drag-and-drop, since that gives users a faster and more flexible way to work with charts. It feels like a practical change that removes friction without changing the core experience too much.

entry.title: Re: Employee Access to Your Onboarding Checklist
entry.content: Thank you for your comments. SAP Support was not able to answer this, or at least not to my satisfaction. All I am seeing in behavior is that the rule to archive triggers at 90 days past start date (this is set at 90 days to drop these hires from the Dashboard). This is the only time the Onboarding Checklist is accessible for the new hire -- after 90 days! The process is not getting completed until this archive job runs - again at 90 days past start date. So, this new feature is very much lacking from my perspective. After 90 days has passed, who cares about seeing this at that time, it's too late. I am now waiting for the new upcoming feature of being able to access documents from the Employee Profile to hopefully see a better experience for our new hries. I assume it ill take multiple releases and multiple issues and enhancements - but in time, I am a hopeful this will become the way to go. I will be patient!

entry.title: Re: Simplified Maintenance Resource Scheduling - RSH (43R) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content: @malaykumarghosh what sort of additional license is needed for this functionality . Is this included with the S4 license? Do we need SAP FSAM license or not to use this?

entry.title: Re: Issue During SAP Certification Exam – Valuation Area...
entry.content:
Hi @PG_Basavachetan 

I reached out to one of our SAP experts and wanted to share the guidance provided—it should help you move forward:

  • Run transaction EC02 and copy an existing plant such as CT00 or 1010.
  • Update the address details for the new plant using OX10.
  • Remove any storage locations that are not required.
  • Verify the activation of Material Ledger via OMX1.
  • Execute CKMSTART to complete the setup.

At this point, most of the configuration should be in place without errors.

Additionally, don’t forget to:

  • Define a Purchasing Organization and assign it to your new plant.
  • Double-check that you are using “0” (zero) instead of the letter “O”, as this can easily cause issues.

Hope this helps—let me know if you have any follow-up questions!

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Managing JMS Queues Programmatically with the new SAP Cloud Integration JMS OData API
entry.content:
@RubikWal 

In some cases (like when a downstream system was down), a large number of messages accumulated in the JMS queue.
When trying to reprocess everything at once, we observed:

  • API timeouts during retry
  • Performance degradation in CPI and target system
  • Difficulty isolating which messages were failing
  • Risk of overwhelming downstream systems

nstead of retrying all messages in one go, we split the backlog based on message creation timestamp (or enqueue time) into smaller chunks.

For example:

  • Retry messages from 10:00–10:10
  • Then 10:10–10:20
  • Then 10:20–10:30

So we process manageable batches instead of full queue at once.

 

  • Prevents system overload
  • Improves retry success rate
  • Makes troubleshooting easier (we know which time range had issues)
  • Gives better control over high-volume recovery

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay: how to correctly configure field format...
entry.content:
Your number for Money fields should be something like this ###,##0.   This brings every entry to a whole number.   There is a setting to round up, down or half up and there is a multiplier option you can set to 1, 10, 100, etc.   For example, if you set the multiplier to 100, you results will always have a value ending in 100.   49,750 would be 49,800 if round up or half up and 49,700 if round down.

The goal section weight is defined in the bonus plan.  you probably have it set to 60 rather than .6.   100 % is configured as 1.

This is all covered in the certification course.   Are you certified in Variable Pay?  If not, you should not be configuring the system without a more experienced colleague.

entry.title: Re: create Service PO using FM BAPI_PO_CREATE1
entry.content:
Hi @Aran1 , please check this post if it helps:

entry.content: Anyone who could help us here in this regards? We would really appreciate hearing about your experiences. Best, Natalie

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud - FAQs
entry.content: of course I did that.  Still there is lot of confusion. If these data products from S4 are to be licensed separately for each LoB then the business case is quite weak. Price tag is quite hefty and each LoB pricing is so varied. Replacing data sources with SAP managed data products will be hard sell when anyway these data products will be enhanced/customized.

entry.title: How Can You Effectively Position SAP Business Network Solutions?
entry.content:
If you want to confidently connect business challenges with the right SAP solutions, the “Positioning SAP Solutions for Business Network Solutions” course is your next step.

 

You’ll learn

This course helps you move beyond product knowledge and focus on real-world application.

You will learn how to:

  • Understand key business challenges and customer needs
  • Map those needs to SAP Business Network solutions
  • Build strong, compelling value narratives
  • Communicate with clarity in customer-facing scenarios

Perfect for learners who want to grow their impact, including:

  • SAP consultants and partners
  • Pre-sales and sales professionals
  • Anyone involved in solution storytelling and positioning

🚀 Build your expertise and elevate your conversations today. access the Positioning SAP Solutions for Business Network Solutions” course

Join the Conversation

How do you approach positioning SAP solutions today? Share your experience or insights—we’re always excited to learn from the community!

entry.title: Re: Error in payment proposal creation
entry.content:
Hi Aish,

As other users have stated, without a clear description of your specific case, it is quite hard to provide you with an exact solution. However, I can give you a few general comments and link you to the official documentation. There are several reasons why a payment may fall into an exception. First, it is important to check the payment proposal log, as it details exactly which rule failed. See this SAP Note if you have issues navigating to the log itself: SAP Note 2858351 

Here is a list of the most common exceptions during a payment proposal creation and what they mean in S/4HANA:

  1. 001 - No pymt possible because Items with a debit bal.still exist: Multiple possible reasons, check the SAP Note 2586464 
  2. 003 - Item is Blocked for Payment: This usually means the invoice has a blocking reason (e.g., 'A' or 'R') assigned either manually by a user or automatically by the system (e.g., due to tolerance limits in Invoice Verification). Such an item needs to be "opened" for payment (the block has to be removed). See also SAP Note 3255442 
  3. 006 - No valid payment method found: This might mean: Missing payment method in BP/vendor master, missing bank details, payment method not configured for house bank. See Sap Note 2671294 
  4. 007 - Error in creating the payment document; read job log: Usually a missing G/L account in account determination (e.g. cash discount G/L). See SAP Note 3678208 

Other common exceptions to be aware of:

  1. Vendor blocked, editing not possible: another user is making changes to the same vendor. The locking user should close the transaction to release the lock.
  2. Vendor blocked due to delete flag or posting block: the vendor has a posting block or deletion flag set. This needs to be resolved in the Business Partner master data.
  3. Minimum Amount Not Reached: The total payment amount is lower than the minimum amount defined for the payment method or house bank configuration.

Also, please check the last point in the list on Sap Help - Manage Automatic Payments where other common errors might be listed.

Hope it helps.

Best regards,

Michal

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC - Electronic Invoicing for Italy : ItalyReceiveNotification without SNI
entry.content: So we try with a reverse proxy, which inserts SNI api.cf.eu20-001.hana.ondemand.com and does a rewrite of HTTP Host from our domain name to api.cf.eu20-001.hana.ondemand.com but it's failed

entry.title: Re: Introducing Our New Reply Progression Badges and a Color Refresh!
entry.content:
"What is your favourite colour?" "Red, no, blue" "<sproing>"--Python's Holy Grail quest stopper.

I vote green, with a caveat to look into text contrast levels, or as I call it, "avoid the grey on grey scheme at all costs!"

I used a tool called "Colour Contrast Analyser" or CCA, version 3.5.4 to check the middle of each range and found only the rightmost column passes all their tests.

(edit: link to the site for the CCA tool: https://github.com/ThePacielloGroup/CCAe )

Contrast check

entry.title: Re: SAP ASE Internals
entry.content:
@Derek_Asirvadem 

First of all, thanks for posting this really useful info.  There isn't that much like it online anymore.   Now I'll ask some questions about what you're saying:

Re: In Threaded mod, ASE runs as a single (unix) process

In my RedHat/Centos version of Linux, when running ASE in threaded mode, I see this in Linux:

> ps -eLf | egrep "PPID|dataserver" | cut -c1-123 | head -5
UID          PID    PPID     LWP  C NLWP STIME TTY          TIME CMD
syblogin   10056   10043   10056  0   21 May07 ?        00:00:30 /myhomedir/bin/dataserver  
syblogin   10056   10043   13287  0   21 May07 ?        00:05:48 /myhomedir/bin/dataserver  
syblogin   10056   10043   13288  0   21 May07 ?        00:00:03 /myhomedir/bin/dataserver  
syblogin   10056   10043   13289  0   21 May07 ?        00:00:02 /myhomedir/bin/dataserver

So yes, ASE is running as a single process (with PID 10056 in this example), but there are clearly Linux light weight processes (LWP) for each thread in ASE.  I'm pretty sure these LWPs weren't used in the old "process mode" (but I don't have any process mode servers running anymore to check this).

My server has threads configured the following way:

myserver > sp_helpthread;
 Name              Type                 CurrentSize TargetSize IdleTimeout Description                                  InstanceName
 ----------------- -------------------- ----------- ---------- ----------- -------------------------------------------- ------------
 syb_blocking_pool Run To Completion              4          4           0 A pool dedicated to executing blocking calls NULL        
 syb_default_pool  Engine (Multiplexed)           8          8         100 The default pool to run query sessions       NULL        
 syb_system_pool   Run To Completion              3          3           0 The I/O and system task pool                 NULL

So sp_helpthread is showing 15 threads.  Interestingly, at the Linux level, I see 21 threads/LWPs.  Probably a few ASE system threads not showing in sp_helpthread, that's fine.

I generally think of lightweight processes in Linux as OS implemented threads.   From what I'm reading online, threads in Linux are actually processes which happen to share some resources with the parent "thread" (see Light-weight process (Wikipedia))

FYI, you can run "top -H" to see ASE activity at the lightweight thread level.

Re: sp_sysmon stuff

Generally speaking, I was trying to say that sp_sysmon shows things from the engine/thread point of view, not from a user task (spid) point of view.  Ie., you can see that the overall ASE server is busy or now, but it's hard to tell what an individual spid is bottlenecked on.

It's possible to get some idea what individual spids are doing using the "appmgmt" option within sp_sysmon:

> sp_sysmon "00:00:15",appmgmt,@applmon='appl_and_login';
...snip...
  Application->Login:   MyApp->MyLogin

  Application Activity            per sec      per xact       count  % of total
  -------------------------  ------------  ------------  ----------
    CPU Busy                          3.1           0.0          46       5.1 %
    I/O Busy                         57.3           0.0         860      94.5 %
    Idle                              0.3           0.0           4       0.4 %

    Number of Times Scheduled      4170.1           3.2       62552       n/a

  Application Priority Changes    per sec      per xact       count  % of total
  -------------------------  ------------  ------------  ----------  ----------
  Total Priority Changes              0.0           0.0           0       n/a

  Application I/Os Completed     per sec      per xact       count  % of total
  -------------------------  ------------  ------------  ----------  ----------
    Disk I/Os Completed            1143.1           0.9       17146      13.1 %
    Network I/Os Completed         7582.0           5.8      113730      86.9 %
  -------------------------  ------------  ------------  ----------
  Total I/Os Completed             8725.1           6.7      130876

  Resource Limits Violated      per sec      per xact       count  % of total
  -------------------------  ------------  ------------  ----------  ----------
  Total Limits Violated               0.0           0.0           0       n/a
...snip...

Where, according to the docs, CPU, I/O, Idle counts are defined as:

  • CPU Busy – reports the number of clock ticks during which the user task was executing during the sample interval. When the numbers in this category are high, it indicates a CPU-bound application. If this is a problem, you may want to consider engine binding.

  • I/O Busy – reports the number of clock ticks during which the user task was performing I/O during the sample interval. If the numbers in this category are high, it indicates an I/O-intensive process. If idle time is also high, the application could be I/O bound.

    The application might achieve better throughput if you assign it a higher priority, bind it to a lightly loaded engine or engine group, or partition the application’s data onto multiple devices.

  • Idle – reports the number of clock ticks during which the user task was idle during the sample interval.

  • Number of Times Scheduled – reports the number of times a user task is scheduled and chosen to run on an engine. This data can help you determine whether an application has sufficient resources. If this number is low for a task that normally requires substantial CPU time, it may indicate insufficient resources. Consider changing priority in a loaded system with sufficient engine resources.

The way I'm reading this, "Idle" ticks only occur for a spid if it's scheduled.  But if the spid just isn't doing anything at all, then I guess it doesn't get any idle ticks??  That's backed up by observations.    The sum of CPU busy + I/O Busy + Idle ticks doesn't add up to a constant number.

I'm not sure if a spid which is scheduled and waiting for an I/O to complete, shows up as I/O busy ticks or Idle ticks.  Maybe I'll add a separate posting about that.

entry.title: Re: how can i check doubled posted and doubled cleared it...
entry.content: somehow it helped but i need more specific way, coz i got a bit confused after sorting and im not sure if i sorted right, can you maybe upload some screenshots to how to sort it with fbl5n?

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: Data Tiering Strategy and Best Practices
entry.content: Thank you @jeet_kapase , for your insightful blog. I would be interested to learn more about your take on dynamic union pruning, as we have in BW. How can we optimize the data selection when the data is only available in one tier and not the other? One option I've seen in the past is to create a control table that specifies where the data resides (by year or by BU, for example) to direct requests to the right path. How did you see this problem handled?

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Dimension Tables Move to the File Repository
entry.content:
@Max_Gander 

This is an important change because moving dimension tables into the file repository simplifies how SAC content is managed and governed. I like that it reduces fragmentation and makes transport handling more predictable, especially for customers dealing with older packages and mixed content. It feels like the kind of platform cleanup that makes administration easier even if it is not flashy on the surface.

entry.title: Register now for the 2608 Early Release Series!
entry.content:
The Early Release Series is a biannual event showcasing live sessions led by product experts. These sessions provide an exclusive preview of upcoming innovations, including AI-driven features and advancements with Joule, tailored to selected lines of business and industries. Spanning several days, the series offers expert demos, insider previews, and real-time Q&A, giving participants a first look at exciting updates and the opportunity to get their questions answered directly.

From July 13 to July 17, 2026, we'll have a number of expert-led live sessions taking place to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the 2608 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you.

Save the date and stay tuned for the registration to open soon!

 

_________________

Sign-up instructions:

An active SAP Learning Hub license is required to register for the live sessions and to consume the recordings. Find out how to sign up

As a customer:

Access to at least the SAP Enterprise Support Edition is required. Follow these steps to sign up:

  1. Navigate to the sign-up page and click on the “sign up” button.
  2. Authenticate yourself with your S-user ID and password.
  3. The system will check your eligibility, create a new SAP Learning Hub user, and populate your learning catalogue.
    This process may take up to 2 hours
  4. You will receive a registration confirmation via e-mail once the registration process is complete.

As a partner:
Partners with a valid SAP Universal ID, who are able to login to the Empowering SAP partners site, will be able to access the live sessions and recordings.

As an SAP internal:
Register here for the SAP Learning Hub Internal Edition (free of charge) by clicking on “access”.

Experiencing issues?
Make sure to be logged on before registering. In case of any other issues, reach out to SAP Cloud ERP Early Release Series

entry.title: Re: SAP ASE Internals
entry.content:
Actually, I'll correct/answer myself in the previous post. 

The help doc says I/O busy ticks are for a user task (spid) "performing I/O" (which is not precisely defined), but if a spid also has idle ticks, then it could be I/O bound.

So this seems to say that a spid waiting for an I/O to complete shows up as "idle"

entry.title: Re: Application Decisions for Sales Order Management in CX - Part 1
entry.content:
@Juliuspereira,

We just released the third part of this series where we focus on S/4HANA Sales; take a look and let me know your thoughts: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-architecture-knowledge-base/application-decisions-for-sales-order-management-part-3/ta-p/14332001

Cheers,

Alvaro

entry.title: Recommendation on Write Variant Date Selection in CV_DVS ILM Archiving
entry.content:
Hi,

Need recommendation on which date field should be used in the write job variant for archiving object CV_DVS when ILM Archiving with Data Destruction option enabled.

In transaction IRM_CUST, under Value Determination for Time References (Indirect), I can see that both ADATUM (Output Date) and VRLDAT (Due Date) from table DRAW are configured by default.

Our requirement is to permanently destroy DMS documents after a retention period of 8 years. Could you please advise which of these date fields should be used as the time reference in the write job variant to correctly calculate the retention expiry date?

Regards,

TY

entry.title: Re: SAP ASE Internals
entry.content:
@Derek_Asirvadem 

This continuation of the SAP ASE internals discussion is valuable because it shows people actually digging into low-level details instead of just talking around the database. I like that the conversation touches on internals topics like signal handling and internal error levels, since those are the kinds of things that matter when you are troubleshooting deep issues. It feels especially relevant for engineers who need to understand ASE behavior beyond the surface-level documentation.

entry.title: Re: SAP Security Configuration Dashboard using SAP Analytics Cloud (Template)
entry.content:
Hello Wihem,

in the query , I replace provider from CCDB_STORE_DATA to PROVIDERS_LIST, it's better now for me.

As when I try here Try Out | SAP Cloud ALM Analytics | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

There is only one provider ROVIDERS_LIST.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 06 - Project specific configuration

@OlgaDolinskaja  Very insightful Blog! Thanks for putting together. Was going through ABAP platform deck and there are few links in Page32 did not map correctly. It says internal server error if I clicked on it. Could you please check and have them corrected? 

Thanks,

Girdhari

entry.title: The Challenge: Large Platform, Demanding Operations
entry.content:
SAP Integration Suite is a powerful platform for building and operating integrations at enterprise scale. Its design environment is feature-rich, its runtime is reliable, and its API coverage is broad. But for teams managing dozens — or hundreds — of integration flows across multiple environments, the operational side of the story can quickly become the bottleneck.

Consider a typical scenario: a new release is ready. The team needs to promote integration packages from development to test and then to production. Each package needs its externalized parameters re-applied for the target environment. Log levels need to be set appropriately. Each flow needs to be triggered for activation. If any object is locked, or if the deployment order is wrong, the team has to intervene manually. And when a flow is retired, there is no mechanism to transport that deletion — someone has to remove it by hand in each environment.

The same challenge applies to Partner Directory entries — a critical configuration object for many integration scenarios, especially pipeline-based architectures. Partner Directory entries have no built-in transport mechanism. Setting them up manually on a production system, under time pressure, with no rollback option, is a risk that teams simply should not have to accept.

None of this is a criticism of the platform. It simply reflects what happens when integration teams grow, release frequencies increase, and the margin for error shrinks. Manual processes that work fine for small teams become fragile at scale.

This is the problem that CICD Actions for SAP Integration Suite was designed to solve.

What Is It?

CICD Actions for SAP Integration Suite is an open-source CI/CD automation layer for SAP Integration Suite, built on GitHub Actions. It was developed jointly by SAP and a customer, born from real operational needs on a large-scale integration landscape. It has been running in production since October 2025 and is available today under the Apache 2.0 license:

https://github.com/SAP/cicd-actions-for-sap-integration-suite

A quick note on GitHub Actions — if you are not familiar with it: GitHub Actions is the built-in automation platform of GitHub. It lets you define workflows that run in response to events (a button click, a schedule, or a code push). No separate CI/CD tool to install. The entire deployment pipeline runs directly inside your GitHub repository.

The Core Idea: Git as the Single Source of Truth

The solution is built on one principle: Git holds everything — not just the integration flow source code, but also the configuration that goes with it: externalized parameter values per environment, deployment configuration per flow, Access Policies, and Partner Directory content.

GitHub Actions then uses this Git-stored configuration to fully automate the path from development to production. Deletions travel through the pipeline. Parameter changes are applied automatically. Log levels are managed after deployment. No manual intervention between environments.

SAP Integration Suite — DEV
     │  Download package as ZIP
     │  Extract + merge configuration
     ▼
Git Repository
     │  Change detection
     │  Environment-specific parameters stored as code
     ▼
SAP Integration Suite — TST / PRD / ...
     │  Upload to Design Time
     │  Activate to Runtime
     │  Apply environment-specific log levels
     ▼
Done — fully automated, fully auditable

Every step is recorded in Git. Every deployment is traceable. Every configuration change is reviewed before it is applied.

The Workflow Library at a Glance

The solution ships as a library of reusable GitHub Actions workflows and composite actions. Teams adopt it through a single sync workflow that pulls the latest templates automatically — when new features are released, your workflows update themselves.

 
Environment dropdowns are populated automatically from your configured GitHub Environments. DEV and TST are required with those exact names; any further stages — such as a pre-production or production environment — can be named freely.

Workflow What It Does

Download → Git → DeployDownload an Integration Package or Partner Directory from DEV, store it in Git, deploy to TST
Deploy / DeleteUpload or delete Integration Packages and Partner Directory entries to any configured environment
Delete from BTP and GitRemove an Integration Package or Partner Directory from all environments and from Git
Download to GitSnapshot Integration Packages or Partner Directory entries from any environment to a Git branch
Release ImportCalculate the delta between two Git refs and deploy changed packages to a target environment
Update iFlow ParametersApply externalized parameter changes to a specific iFlow and optionally redeploy
Access Policies (3 workflows)Download, upload, and manage Access Policies across environments
Dashboard GeneratorGenerate the operations dashboard
Create ReleaseVersioned release management from the development branch
 

Every workflow that operates on Integration Packages also supports Partner Directory entries — selected via a single mode input.

What's New in v1.3.0

Version 1.3.0 is the largest release to date.

The Dashboard — See Your Entire Landscape at a Glance

The headline feature is the Integration Suite Operations Dashboard: a static HTML page, automatically generated by a scheduled workflow and published to GitHub Pages. It shows every integration package and every IFlow across all configured environments — runtime status, deployed version, version drift between stages, and whether the package is managed by the pipeline. Color-coded health indicators make problem areas immediately visible.

The dashboard gives an immediate overview of every integration package across all environments — version drift, deployment errors, and unmanaged packages are visible at a glance.

Expanding a package reveals the full detail. Two typical situations a team encounters during release work:

Version drift — both IFlows are running a newer version on DEV and TST than on PRD. Typical during an active release: the new version has been promoted to test but not yet to production. Missing deployment — the second IFlow is configured for production but shows — in the PRD runtime column: it has not been imported yet. The deployment configuration says it should be there; BTP says it is not. Without this dashboard, that gap would likely go unnoticed until a runtime error surfaces.

The dashboard is regenerated daily and can be triggered manually at any time. Self-contained HTML — no server required.

GitHub App Authentication

v1.3.0 replaces Personal Access Tokens with GitHub App authentication. Two dedicated apps handle the read and write sides of the pipeline with minimal, explicit permissions. Token rotation is automatic.

Access Policies as Code

Access Policies can now be version-controlled and promoted through the pipeline. The pattern is identical to integration packages: download from DEV, commit to Git, review, deploy.

Automatic Log Level Management

The deployment pipeline now automatically sets iFlow log levels to an elevated setting before deployment and resets them to the configured operational level afterwards. No manual navigation to the operations cockpit after every release.

A Different Approach to Integration Lifecycle Management

 
SAP provides transport and lifecycle management capabilities for Integration Suite. This solution takes a different path: Git becomes the system of record, GitHub Actions drives the entire lifecycle, and every operation — download, deploy, delete, configure — is automated and auditable. For teams that have adopted a DevOps mindset and want full control over their integration pipeline, this is a deliberate alternative rather than an add-on.

Built Together, Open Sourced Together

This solution was developed collaboratively by SAP and a customer to reduce the manual overhead of release operations on a large enterprise integration landscape. It is available to any team, free of charge, under the Apache 2.0 license, and works on both GitHub Enterprise Cloud and GitHub Enterprise Server.

What's Coming in This Series

  1. The Download → Git → Deploy pipeline — what actually happens, step by step
  2. Managing parameters and log levels — environment-specific configuration as code
  3. The Release Import workflow — controlled promotion using Git tags and branches
  4. Access Policies as code — governance and auditability
  5. The Dashboard in depth — how to read it, configure it, and act on what it shows
  6. Extending the pipeline with Customer Exits — inject your own tests and deployment rules without touching the core

Repository and documentation: https://github.com/SAP/cicd-actions-for-sap-integration-suite

entry.title: Re: Utilities Customer Self-Service Agent: Reduce Operational Costs and Enhance Customer Experience
entry.content:
Hi @Ivo_Kulhavy ,

We currently support all languages support by GPT-4o.

Regards

Bruno

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 01 - Common types and aspects
Re: Exclusive webinars and enablement resources for SAP Partners
entry.content:
@DamienB 

This is a useful resource for SAP partners because it gives them a clear path to exclusive webinars and enablement they would not find elsewhere. I like that the content is organized specifically for partners, since that makes it easier to stay up to date without having to search through general SAP materials. It feels like a practical way to support partner growth and keep skills aligned with what SAP is actually delivering.

entry.title: Re: Strategic Data Paradigms for building AI-ready IT Systems
entry.content:
@VenkatG 

This is a useful framing because it connects high-level data paradigms like Data Fabric, Data Mesh, and Knowledge Graphs to the practical goal of making IT systems AI-ready. I like that the content treats these paradigms as strategic building blocks rather than buzzwords, since that helps data professionals see how to apply them in real architectures. It feels especially relevant for teams trying to plan data foundations that will actually support AI work, not just analytics.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Assign House Bank Account ID (GIRO) in S/4H...
entry.content:
House Bank GR01 / D-B52: Locate/Create the Bank Account Number

  • The bank account number is stored under: Company Code → House Banks → Bank Accounts (identified by an Account ID).

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 02 - Content for common reuse types
Re: What is a Data Steward?
entry.content:
@Corrie 

This is a clear and practical explanation of the data steward role, especially because it highlights both the business and technical responsibilities. I like that the content shows how data stewards act as a bridge between teams, since that is often the most important part of the job in real organizations. It feels like a useful starting point for anyone trying to understand what stewardship means in practice, not just as a title.

entry.title: Re: DEMO: Conversational Analytics with SAP Analytics Cloud in Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
@ThierryAudas 

This demo is a great example of how conversational analytics makes SAC more accessible for business users. I like that the video shows the "Just Ask" natural language query feature in action, because that is where the value really shows up for people who do not want to build formulas or complex reports. It feels especially relevant for teams that want users to explore data on their own while still getting trustworthy, visual answers.
